name: Canadian Cipate (C-pout)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cube the meats and place with one-third of the onions in a 5-quart roasting pan.
2. Add a layer of vegetables and spices, then cover the first layer of meat and vegetables with pastry, cutting openings in the pastry for steam to escape.
3. Continue layers in this manner twice more, always finishing with a layer of dough.
4. Pour wine and water into the dough incisions.
5. Cover tightly and cook for 4 hours in a 300°F oven.
6. Serve during Christmas, mainly in French-speaking areas of Canada.
